content.text:
{For nothing will be impossible with God.}{Luke 1:37}
 {The imaginary number is a fine and wonderful recourse of the divine spirit.}{— Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz}
The characteristic polynomial may have complex roots even for a real matrix.
Recall: $i = \sqrt{-1}$, and complex numbers have form $a + bi$.

content.title: Complex eigenvalues
content.solution:
$\det(A - \lambda I) = \lambda^2 + 1 = 0 \implies \lambda = \pm i$.

The eigenvectors involve complex entries:
For $\lambda = i$: $\vect{v} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ -i \end{bmatrix}$.

content.title: Complex Eigenvalues of Real Matrices
content.content:
If $A$ is a real matrix and $\lambda = a + bi$ is an eigenvalue 
(with $b \neq 0$), then $\bar{\lambda} = a - bi$ is also an 
eigenvalue, with corresponding eigenvector $\bar{\vect{v}}$.
content.label: Theorem

content.text:
A $2 \times 2$ real matrix with complex eigenvalues $a \pm bi$ 
acts as a rotation-scaling: rotation by $\theta = \arctan(b/a)$ 
and scaling by $r = \sqrt{a^2 + b^2}$.

marginNotes.text:
Have you entered into the springs of the 
sea, or walked in the recesses of the deep?
